core_mechanism: Conditioning on random outcomes measured on a shared intermediary induces statistical dependence (correlation) between two otherwise causally-disconnected subsystems -- a conditioning-induced coupling ('teleportation') channel whose onset timescale grows logarithmically with the separation across the intermediary.
brief_reason: The abstract states that projective measurements on the common environment generate entanglement between two disjoint, causally-disconnected subregions and that the timescales for both this measurement-induced teleportation and the resulting universal-ensemble emergence scale logarithmically with the distance separating the subregions.
